content:
Main scheme is an encounter card type representing the villain's primary objective. During step one of the villain phase, place threat indicated in the main scheme's acceleration field (modified by active acceleration tokens/icons). If threat equals or exceeds target threat value, the main scheme is completed and the deck advances; if the villain completes the final stage, the villain wins the game. When the main scheme deck advances:

  1. Remove the top card from the game (return tokens except acceleration to pool, discard attached cards).
  2. Resolve 'When Revealed' on the 'A' side of the new top card.
  3. Flip to 'B' side, place threat equal to starting threat, resolve 'When Revealed' on that side. Excess threat does not carry over to the new stage; acceleration tokens on it do carry over. Main scheme cards cannot be discarded from play.

'Max' and 'maximum' impose a maximum across all copies of a card (by title) for all players. 'Max X per [period]' limits plays during a period (canceled cards still count). 'Max X per deck' restricts copies per player deck. 'Max 1 per player' restricts copies each player may control in play (cannot take control of another copy already controlled). 'Max 1 per [game element]' restricts copies attached to each indicated element. 'Max 1 per [instance]' restricts triggers per single triggering instance across all copies. '(to a maximum of [value])' imposes a maximum on that ability for its duration.
A character's maximum hit points is their base hit points plus or minus all 'gets' hit point modifiers active on that character.
The word 'may' indicates a specified player has the option to resolve the text that follows. If no player is specified, the option is granted to the controller of the card with the ability.
A mental resource is one of the four types of resources. Mental resources can be spent to pay resource costs; some card abilities specifically require them.

Minion is an encounter card type representing supporters of the villain and/or enemies of the heroes.

  • If a minion enters play, it engages the player revealing/putting it into play, unless specified otherwise.
  • If it enters play, it remains until a card ability/game effect causes it to leave; zero or fewer hit points defeats and discards it. Minions engaged with a player activate (one at a time, in the engaged player's chosen order) during step two of the villain phase, after the villain activates.
  • If a minion engages a player during an activation in which all minions engaged with that player are instructed to activate, the newly-engaged minion also activates.

Before starting, players can customize their experience by choosing/combining modes: Standard Mode (basic mode); Expert Mode (adds Expert villain stages and the Expert encounter set, combinable with heroic/skirmish/campaign); Heroic Mode (players choose a heroic level; deal X additional encounter cards each villain phase, combinable with others); Skirmish/Rookie Mode (choose one villain version, remove the others from the game, combinable with others); Campaign Mode (interconnected scenarios with a campaign log; expert campaign mode is the advanced variant; if a card is removed from a campaign it cannot be used for the rest of the campaign, even on retry; players choose other modes per scenario within a campaign).

• The game constantly checks and updates the count of any variable quantity being modified; whenever a modifier is applied or removed, the entire quantity is recalculated from the unmodified base value and all active modifiers.
  • The per-player icon is applied before any modifiers and is not itself considered a modifier.
  • All modifiers are treated as applied simultaneously, but additive/subtractive modifiers are calculated before doubling/halving modifiers.
  • A value 'set' to a specific number overrides all non-set modifiers (most recently resolved set modifier wins conflicts).
  • After all modifiers, a value below zero is treated as zero (no negative icons/attributes/traits/cost/keywords). Fractional values round up after all modifiers.
  • A dash (–) value cannot be modified.

• Cards belonging to a 'modular encounter set' (or 'modular set') are a classification that can be added to/removed from nearly any scenario. Scenarios instruct how many modular sets to include; some required, some chosen.
  • If added, it's added as an entire set — individual cards cannot be included without the rest, unless scenario rules state otherwise. Designated by the modular set's name printed at the bottom of the card.

• Some abilities allow players to move game elements (cards, damage, threat).
  • An element cannot move to its same current placement.
  • If there's no valid source or destination, the move cannot be made.
  • Damage can move between dials and cards (and vice versa), respecting the card's maximum hit points.
  • Damage moved off a character is considered healed; moved to a character is considered dealt. Threat moved off a scheme is considered removed; moved to a scheme is considered placed.
